keeper: G900 selects the carrier packet. G60 supplies the true opposite. The fork decides whether the opposite can be received.

bridge_schema.residue_comparison: rho_21(cycle) compared with support parity of Q_60 versus Q_60_star

role_contracts.P_21: A bounded Project 21 source packet derived from native state, transition, and cycle provenance.
role_contracts.R_G900: A finite carrier-incidence successor rule that selects an existing target packet without hand-picking.
role_contracts.Q_60: A target packet whose members resolve to existing G60 carrier states or edges.
role_contracts.alpha_60: The unique distance-6 antipode involution on G60.
role_contracts.F: The fork or chamber context that determines whether the antipodal counterpart is support-visible.

proof_obligations.statement: Derive P_21 from Project 21 provenance without using answer labels or downstream native targets.
Derive a deterministic R_G900 that selects Q_60 from P_21 over existing carrier incidence.
Resolve every selected Q_60 member to a native G60 state or edge.
Apply alpha_60 and derive the fork context that admits or rejects the antipodal counterpart.
Show that the resulting binary support class agrees with rho_21 on the full seven-dimensional Project 21 cycle space.
Only after B1-B5, test projection through G30 and G15 against the imported native signing.

negative_constraints: Co-visibility of packet components is not accepted as a coupling law.
The local+30 rule is not accepted as the universal native G60 antipode.
A direct Project21-to-G15 edge identity is not assumed.
An abstract rank-one functional match is not treated as native signed-lift identification.
boundary: The existing G900 successor rule is an example, not yet a Project 21 rule.
The true G60 antipode is retained; local+30 is not substituted.
Fork support remains contextual and must be derived.
No G30 or G15 projection is claimed.
No native signed-lift identification, theorem, GR, physics, force, body mutation, or Gap A closure claim.
